function.name: example_add
function.description:
Exposes a calculation tool that adds two numbers together.
    Validates inputs, delegates calculation and database recording to CalculationService,
    and returns the result.

ExampleAddRequest.description: Pydantic schema representing the request parameters for the example_add tool.
ExampleAddRequest.title: ExampleAddRequest

function.name: get_ecommerce_user
function.description:
Queries the ECommerceUser database table to search for user metadata by email.
    
    If the user exists, returns user profile, status, and creation time.
    If the user does not exist, returns exists=False. You should check the 'exists' 
    field first before describing the user status to the customer.

GetECommerceUserRequest.description: Pydantic schema representing the request parameters to query ECommerceUser.

email.description: The unique email of the user
email.title: Email

GetECommerceUserRequest.title: GetECommerceUserRequest

function.name: get_ecommerce_orders
function.description:
Queries the ECommerceOrder database table to search for all orders by customer email.
    
    Returns a list of order records sorted by creation timestamp descending.
    If no orders are found, returns an empty list.

GetECommerceOrdersRequest.description: Pydantic schema representing the request parameters to query ECommerceOrders.

email.description: The email address of the customer to query orders for
email.title: Email

GetECommerceOrdersRequest.title: GetECommerceOrdersRequest

function.name: get_ecommerce_order_details
function.description:
Queries the database to retrieve metadata and the itemized product list of an order by order_id.
    
    If the order does not exist, returns exists=False. You should check the 'exists'
    field first before describing the order details.

GetECommerceOrderDetailsRequest.description: Pydantic schema representing the request parameters to query ECommerceOrder details.

order_id.description: The unique ID of the order to retrieve details for
order_id.title: Order Id

GetECommerceOrderDetailsRequest.title: GetECommerceOrderDetailsRequest

function.name: get_return_requests_by_order
function.description:
Queries return requests associated with a specific order_id.
    
    Returns the matching return request record if found.

GetReturnRequestsByOrderRequest.description: Pydantic schema representing the request parameters to query return requests by order_id.

order_id.description: The unique ID of the order to query returns for
order_id.title: Order Id

GetReturnRequestsByOrderRequest.title: GetReturnRequestsByOrderRequest

function.name: get_return_requests_by_customer
function.description:
Queries return requests associated with a specific customer_id (user_id).
    
    Returns a list of matching return request records, sorted by creation time descending.

GetReturnRequestsByCustomerRequest.description: Pydantic schema representing the request parameters to query return requests by customer_id.

customer_id.description: The unique ID of the customer to query returns for
customer_id.title: Customer Id

GetReturnRequestsByCustomerRequest.title: GetReturnRequestsByCustomerRequest

function.name: create_ecommerce_return_request
function.description:
Creates a new customer return request record in the database.
    
    Returns the created return request details upon success.

CreateReturnRequestInput.description: Pydantic schema representing the input to create a return request.

order_id.description: The unique ID of the order to return
order_id.title: Order Id

customer_id.description: The ID of the customer associated with the return
customer_id.title: Customer Id

reason_code.description: The reason code for the return

item_condition.description: The condition of the product being returned

created_by.description: The user_id of the agent who operates this return request creation
created_by.title: Created By

CreateReturnRequestInput.title: CreateReturnRequestInput
